name: Lemon Chicken
recipeInstructions:
1. For the marinade: In a small bowl, combine 1 tablespoon vegetable oil, cornstarch dissolved in water, 2 tablespoons soy sauce, and 1/2 tablespoon sherry. Rub the mixture over chicken halves, allowing excess to drain off. Lightly coat with additional cornstarch. Refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
2. For the lemon sauce: In a small saucepan, combine 2 cups bean sprouts, 2 cups snow peas, 3/4 cup water chestnuts, and 3 tablespoons sugar. Add 3 teaspoons catsup (or ketchup), juice from 1 lemon, and zest of 1 lemon. Cook over medium-high heat until the vegetables are crisp-tender. Dissolve 1 teaspoon cornstarch in 1/2 cup water, then stir into the sauce.
3. Heat 1/2 tablespoon vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Fry chicken halves until golden brown on both sides and cooked through. Remove from skillet and set aside.
4. Add remaining vegetable oil to the skillet. Stir-fry bean sprouts, snow peas, water chestnuts, and sliced water chestnuts for 1-2 minutes until crisp-tender.
5. Divide vegetables among serving platters. Top with chicken pieces and spoon lemon sauce over the top. Garnish with lemon wedges, green onion slices, and chopped almonds. Serve hot.
